content: The original Self-Refine framework's single-perspective iterative feedback loop can be augmented by integrating the multiple inconsistent solving perspectives central to recent LLM self-reflection mechanisms, creating a multi-perspective self-refinement framework that retains Self-Refine's core advantage of requiring no extra training or supervision. This framework challenges the default assumption that self-feedback has to come from a single unified model state, unlocking better refinement performance by leveraging the model's own divergent outputs as a source of constructive feedback.
